mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE > 4.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust household balance sheets and construction activity, which amplifies aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends coincide with negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions yield negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL > 295.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real existing home sales confirms broad-based demand recovery across goods and housing sectors, which sustains corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, which triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to recession fears.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases yield positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases.
